2016-09-25.abstract: The telescope, which officially began operating on Sunday in a majestic but poor part of Guizhou Province, embodies China’s ambitions as a scientific power.

2016-09-25.title: Writer Charged With Insulting Islam Is Killed as Extremism Boils Over in Jordan
2016-09-25.abstract: Nahed Hattar, who was recently arrested after sharing a cartoon on Facebook that offended some Muslims, was killed outside a court in Amman.

2016-09-25.title: Activist in South Korea Dies of Injuries From Police Water Cannon
2016-09-25.abstract: The activist, Baek Nam-gi, who had been expelled from school twice for protests against the dictator Park Chung-hee, was injured while opposing Mr. Park’s daughter, President Park Geun-hye.

2016-09-26.title: Socialist Party Sees Deep Losses in Spain Elections
2016-09-26.abstract: The regional votes have implications on whether the country’s four national parties can end the nine-month deadlock in Spain’s national politics.

2016-09-26.title: Mexico Extinguishes Fuel Tanker Fire Off Gulf Coast
2016-09-26.abstract: Crew members were safely evacuated and no extensive spill had been detected, but the episode was another blow to the state oil company.

2016-09-26.title: Two Years After a Night of Horror, Mexican Students Seek Answers
2016-09-26.abstract: The families of 43 kidnapped college students continue to lobby the government for justice — accompanied by students who lived through the 2014 attack.

2016-09-26.title: To the Moon, North Korea? Or Does a Rocket Have a Darker Aim?
2016-09-26.abstract: The North explains a rocket engine test as a moonshot, but intelligence agencies are exploring another option: the beginnings of a missile that could reach the American mainland.

2016-09-26.title: Watching Finland’s Pesapallo
2016-09-26.abstract: Pesapallo, often called Finland's national game, is similar to baseball. But watch, and you'll see the difference.

2016-09-27.title: Decline in Oil Prices Lands on Government Workers as Saudi Arabia Cuts Paychecks
2016-09-27.abstract: The reductions affect a wide range of government employees, from ministers, whose pay was cut by 20 percent, to civil servants, whose overtime pay and vacation time will face new limits.

2016-09-27.title: U.S. Says Chinese Executive Helped North Korea Dodge Sanctions
2016-09-27.abstract: An indictment against Ma Xiaohong, her company and three colleagues accuses them of violating American bans and helping Pyongyang develop nuclear arms.

2016-09-27.title: Women Petition U.N. Leader to Seek Korean Peace Treaty
2016-09-27.abstract: More than 130 activists from 38 countries urged Ban Ki-moon to pursue a permanent peace treaty to end the Korean War, replacing the 63-year-old armistice.

2016-09-27.title: After Mudslides and Flooding in Iceland, Elves Are Suspects
2016-09-27.abstract: Workers clearing a roadway after a series of storms unleashed havoc after unwittingly dumping dirt on a special rock that has its own name in Icelandic folklore.

2016-09-27.title: Prison Sentence Over Smashing of Shrines in Timbuktu: 9 Years
2016-09-27.abstract: The decision, by judges at the International Criminal Court in The Hague, was the body’s first prosecution of the destruction of cultural heritage as a war crime.

2016-09-28.title: Concern as Venezuela Refuses to Accept Aid
2016-09-28.abstract: The State Department said that John Kerry’s meeting with President Nicolás Maduro of Venezuela was Mr. Kerry’s chance to express concern about “challenges that have affected millions of Venezuelans.”

2016-09-28.title: Max Mannheimer, a Keeper of Holocaust Memory, Dies at 96
2016-09-28.abstract: Mr. Mannheimer visited hundreds of schools and other institutions, advocating democracy and helping ensure that Germany’s World War II horrors were never repeated.

2016-09-28.title: Obama Nominates First Ambassador to Cuba in Over 50 Years
2016-09-28.abstract: The president selected Jeffrey DeLaurentis, who has served since 2014 as the chief of mission for the United States in Havana, to fill the post.

2016-09-28.title: Shimon Peres, an Enduring Pillar From Israel’s Founding Era, Dies at 93
2016-09-28.abstract: Mr. Peres helped build up his country’s military might, then worked as hard to establish peace with Israel’s Arab neighbors.

2016-09-28.title: The Life of Shimon Peres
2016-09-28.abstract: One of the last of Israel’s founding generation, Mr. Peres was on the public stage during the country’s seven decades.

2016-09-29.title: Leader of Spain’s Socialist Party Survives Ouster Attempt
2016-09-29.abstract: The future of the main opposition party’s leader, Pedro Sánchez, still hangs in the balance, after top party officials said they would resign in protest.

2016-09-29.title: Parsing What Makes a Trump Supporter Tick
2016-09-29.abstract: Readers emailing from abroad are surprised by the campaign’s bluster. They don’t understand how a race for the American presidency can be dominated by arguments over “birtherism” and beauty queens.

2016-09-29.title: Report Accuses Sudan of ‘Scorched Earth’ Tactics in Darfur
2016-09-29.abstract: Amnesty International said government forces had laid waste to villages this year in an area known as Jebel Marra, burning homes, looting livestock and raping women.

2016-09-29.title: North Korean Soldier Crosses DMZ to Defect, South Says
2016-09-29.abstract: South Korea said officials were debriefing the soldier to determine his motive for leaving the North.

2016-09-29.title: Medicinal Herb or Myth? Indian Official Proposes Hunt for Sanjivani of Lore
2016-09-29.abstract: A state official wants to spend nearly $4 million in search of the plant, which is said to grow in the Himalayas and which a Hindu epic claims has the power to revive the dying.

2016-09-30.title: What Is Russia Up To, and Is It Time to Draw the Line?
2016-09-30.abstract: Russian airstrikes in Syria and cyberattacks have echoed some of the Cold War’s uglier moments. American intelligence officials wonder whether a grander scheme is at work.

2016-09-30.title: A Call for Review of Chinese Purchases of U.S. Companies
2016-09-30.abstract: Members of Congress have urged the Government Accountability Office to review the effectiveness of the Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States (CFIUS). In a letter, they called for greater scrutiny of Chinese corporate purchases, including movie theaters and studios.

2016-09-30.title: Rosetta Mission Ends With Spacecraft’s Dive Into Comet
2016-09-30.abstract: The vessel shut down and landed with a soft belly-flop onto Comet 67P/Churyumov-Gerasimenko at a speed of two miles per hour.

2016-09-30.title: World Leaders Gather to Mourn Shimon Peres, and Possibly His Dream
2016-09-30.abstract: President Obama and others paid homage to Mr. Peres’s tenacious search for reconciliation, yet the service made clear how elusive that idea has actually become in the Middle East.

2016-09-30.title: Chinese Purchases of U.S. Companies Have Some in Congress Raising Eyebrows
2016-09-30.abstract: Sixteen members of Congress are raising questions over the foreign acquisition of American movie theaters and studios.
